tools.apps_review_desc: Mainstream 2FA Applications Review and Anti-Pitfall Guide

2fas.pros: Open source, fully local-first, supports secure iCloud/G-Drive sync.
2fas.cons: Not a true standalone desktop app; you must scan a QR code on PC to use the browser extension.
2fas.summary: Highly trusted local-first 2FA, though the PC experience can be slightly tedious.

aegis.pros: Fully open source, heavy local encryption, wildly powerful export & format compatibility.
aegis.cons: Android-exclusive, lacks native cross-platform cloud sync or an iOS version.
aegis.summary: The absolute top choice for Android privacy geeks and power users.

ente.pros: Fully open source, End-to-End Encrypted cloud sync, offers native desktop apps and a web vault.
ente.cons: Since all data relies on cloud sync, it may lack the purity sought by offline-only purists.
ente.summary: The rapidly growing king of cross-platform sync, serving as Authy's perfect open-source successor.

proton.pros: Deeply integrated into the highly private Proton ecosystem, E2EE sync.
proton.cons: Tied to Proton Pass ecosystem lock-in.
proton.summary: Excellent choice for existing Proton ecosystem users with top-tier privacy.

bitwarden.pros: Open source and free, backed by the reputable Bitwarden security team.
bitwarden.cons: The standalone authenticator app is fairly basic right now (though BW main app also does 2FA).
bitwarden.summary: Pure and solid app endorsed by a reputable giant, ideal for minimalists.

google.pros: Extremely lightweight, works out of the box, offers highly convenient QR batch export, and now supports cloud sync.
google.cons: Closed source. Early sync lacked E2EE (though improved recently). No official desktop app.
google.security: Initial cloud sync was criticized for lacking E2EE, but this has been addressed.
google.summary: A pure, widely-adopted foundation app. Its QR batch export is a killer feature.

microsoft.pros: If you are deep in the Microsoft ecosystem, it supports passwordless logins seamlessly.
microsoft.cons: Severely bloated, forces MS account binding, absolutely refuses standard exports (locking you in), and backups cannot cross iOS/Android.
microsoft.security: Numerous user reports of complete data loss when restoring on a new device, posing a significant security risk.
microsoft.summary: Way too heavy for pure TOTP usage, and its aggressive 'no-export' data monopoly is repulsive (avoid).

freeotp.pros: Open source project maintained by Red Hat, pure and free.
freeotp.cons: Extremely outdated UI, feels abandoned with rare updates.
freeotp.summary: A nostalgic open-source tool, but no longer fits modern daily usage.

authy.pros: Pioneer in cloud sync, very convenient historically.
authy.cons: Closed source, forces phone number binding, holds keys hostage (no exports), desktop apps shut down.
authy.security: Parent company Twilio suffered severe hacker breaches leaking sensitive data.
authy.summary: Once a king, now an absolutely anti-recommended app due to privacy and lock-in risks.

lastpass.pros: Convenient for users deeply tied into LastPass ecosystem.
lastpass.cons: Closed-source cloud architecture, intertwined with LastPass infrastructure.
lastpass.security: LastPass suffered catastrophic global data breaches recently.
lastpass.summary: Due to LastPass's severe reputation crisis, avoid entrusting critical 2FA here.

raivo.pros: Was previously the most recommended open-source iOS 2FA app.
raivo.cons: Recently acquired by a shady shell company (Mobime).
raivo.security: Disastrous update randomly deleted all local seeds for huge amounts of users, unrecoverable.
raivo.summary: A catastrophic tale of trust betrayal and data destruction. DO NOT USE.

api_errors.invalid_secret_format: Invalid secret format (must be Base32, at least 16 chars)
api_errors.missing_service_account: Service and Account names are required
api_errors.export_password_length: Export password needs at least 12 characters
api_errors.missing_content_type: Content and type cannot be empty

api_errors.backup_password_required: Auto backup password is required (at least 12 characters)

api_errors.oauth_state_invalid: Invalid or missing OAuth state parameter. Security check failed.
api_errors.not_whitelisted: Access whitelist not configured. Login denied.
api_errors.telegram_missing_bot_name: Telegram Bot Name is not configured
api_errors.telegram_missing_query: Telegram provider requires full query parameters, not just code

healthCheck.title: Intercept: Security Check Failed
healthCheck.subtitle: We detected critical vulnerabilities or incomplete initialization in the environment configuration. System access is locked to protect your data.
healthCheck.passed_subtitle: Excellent! Your 2FAuth Worker security defense is operating at optimal conditions.

encryption_key_too_short.title: Encryption Key Compliance Error (ENCRYPTION_KEY)
encryption_key_too_short.desc: Your ENCRYPTION_KEY is empty or shorter than 32 characters! This key encrypts all vault seeds and is highly susceptible to brute-forcing.

jwt_secret_too_short.title: Weak Session Secret (JWT_SECRET)
jwt_secret_too_short.desc: Your JWT_SECRET is shorter than 32 characters. Hackers could forge identity credentials and bypass logins.

oauth_allow_all_enabled.title: CRITICAL: Global Access Enabled
oauth_allow_all_enabled.desc: OAUTH_ALLOW_ALL=true is active! This is not a drill. Anyone visiting the site can instantly govern all your 2FA data!

allowed_users_empty.title: Login Whitelist is Empty
allowed_users_empty.desc: The system is locked but OAUTH_ALLOWED_USERS is empty. Nobody can successfully login to this system.

github_config_incomplete.title: Incomplete GitHub Config
github_config_incomplete.desc: You configured GitHub parameters but are missing the Client ID, Secret, or Redirect URI.

telegram_config_incomplete.title: Incomplete Telegram Config
telegram_config_incomplete.desc: Missing Bot Name or Bot Token.

no_provider_configured.title: No OAuth Provider Configured
no_provider_configured.desc: You must configure at least one active OAuth2 provider (like GitHub or Telegram) to access the UI.

suggestions.suggestion_deploy_by_worker: One-click Deploy to Worker: Go to Cloudflare Dashboard -> Workers & Pages -> 2fauth-worker -> Settings -> Variables and Secrets
suggestions.suggestion_deploy_by_gitaction: Deploy via Github Action: Go to Forked Repository -> Settings -> Secrets and variables -> Actions
suggestions.suggestion_deploy_by_docker: Deploy via Docker Compose: Edit docker-compose.yml -> environment
suggestions.encryption_key_suggestion: Please copy the high-strength random key pre-generated for you below, and set it as ENCRYPTION_KEY according to your deployment method.
suggestions.jwt_secret_suggestion: Please copy the high-strength random key pre-generated for you below, and set it as JWT_SECRET according to your deployment method.
suggestions.oauth_allow_all_suggestion: Depending on your deployment method, remove OAUTH_ALLOW_ALL or set its value to false.
suggestions.allowed_users_suggestion: Depending on your deployment method, fill in OAUTH_ALLOWED_USERS.
suggestions.github_config_suggestion: Depending on your deployment method, fill in OAUTH_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, OAUTH_GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET, and OAUTH_GITHUB_REDIRECT_URI.
suggestions.telegram_config_suggestion: Please check the bot configuration in BotFather to ensure the information is complete, and depending on your deployment method, fill in OAUTH_TELEGRAM_BOT_NAME, OAUTH_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, and OAUTH_TELEGRAM_REDIRECT_URI.
suggestions.google_config_suggestion: Please check the credentials configuration in Google Cloud Console, and depending on your deployment method, fill in OAUTH_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, OAUTH_GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, and OAUTH_GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI.
suggestions.nodeloc_config_suggestion: Please check your developer credentials in NodeLoc panel, and depending on your deployment method, fill in OAUTH_NODELOC_CLIENT_ID, OAUTH_NODELOC_CLIENT_SECRET, and OAUTH_NODELOC_REDIRECT_URI.
suggestions.gitee_config_suggestion: Please verify Gitee developer credentials, and depending on your deployment method, fill in OAUTH_GITEE_CLIENT_ID, OAUTH_GITEE_CLIENT_SECRET, and OAUTH_GITEE_REDIRECT_URI.
suggestions.cloudflare_config_suggestion: Requires four parameters: Client ID, Secret, Org Domain, Redirect URI. Depending on your deployment method, fill in OAUTH_CLOUDFLARE_CLIENT_ID, OAUTH_CLOUDFLARE_CLIENT_SECRET, and OAUTH_CLOUDFLARE_REDIRECT_URI.
suggestions.no_provider_suggestion: Consult the README to pick the easiest provider. Retrieve the Client ID and Secret and configure them in Cloudflare's dashboard.

healthCheck.copy_unsupported: Your browser doesn't support automatic copying, please manually select it.
healthCheck.connection_failed: Cannot connect to server for health check

security.no_passkeys: You haven't registered any Passkeys yet

security.add_dialog_tip: Give your device a recognizable name (e.g., My iPhone 15).
